metabolism.name: Nitric Oxide Modulation
metabolism.detail: Activates Src-Caveolin-1-eNOS phosphorylation pathway for vascular function and tissue perfusion.

metabolism.name: eNOS Phosphorylation
metabolism.detail: Endothelial nitric oxide synthase activation leads to vasodilation and improved blood flow to repair sites.

growth.name: GHR Upregulation
growth.detail: Increases growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts, amplifying sensitivity to healing signals even after peptide clearance.

growth.name: FAK-Paxillin Pathway
growth.detail: Focal adhesion kinase activation promotes cell adhesion, spreading, and organized tissue repair.

proliferation.name: Fibroblast Proliferation
proliferation.detail: Promotes fibroblast migration and proliferation essential for connective tissue repair.

proliferation.name: Organized Angiogenesis (Egr-1/NAB2)
proliferation.detail: The Egr-1/NAB2 gene loop acts as both accelerator and brake for blood vessel formation, preventing aberrant growth.

proliferation.name: Re-epithelialization
proliferation.detail: Accelerates keratinocyte migration and epithelial coverage of wound surfaces.

cancer.name: Angiogenesis Control
cancer.detail: Egr-1/NAB2 'master switch' prevents uncontrolled neovascularization that could promote tumor growth.

central_dogma.name: Transcription Factor Activation
central_dogma.detail: Rapidly activates c-Fos, c-Jun, and Egr-1 transcription factors within first hour; gene expression changes persist for weeks despite short peptide half-life.

immune_function.name: Anti-inflammatory
immune_function.detail: Reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines and enhances antioxidant enzyme systems to manage tissue inflammation.

cytokines.name: TNF-α ↓
cytokines.detail: Significant reduction of tumor necrosis factor alpha in inflamed tissues.

cytokines.name: IL-1β ↓
cytokines.detail: Interleukin-1 beta suppression reduces the acute inflammatory response.

cytokines.name: IL-6 ↓
cytokines.detail: Interleukin-6 reduction modulates both local and systemic inflammatory signaling.

human_use.summary: No human clinical trials completed or published as of 2026. BPC-157 is derived from a protein found in human gastric juice, giving it theoretical translational relevance. Anecdotal reports in biohacking communities describe use for tendon injuries, GI conditions, and post-surgical recovery, but no peer-reviewed human data exists. Not approved for human use by any regulatory agency.

animal_models.summary: Over 100 preclinical studies, primarily in rat models. Demonstrates 2–3× faster healing in tendon, ligament, muscle, and bone injury models. Protects against NSAID-induced gastric lesions, alcohol toxicity, and IBD-like conditions. Shows cardiac arrhythmia elimination, TBI neuroprotection, and peripheral nerve regeneration. Oral bioavailability maintained in gastric juice — unique among peptides.

citations.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 in trials for inflammatory bowel disease
Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing

performance.summary: Widely studied in athletic and biohacking communities for accelerated recovery from tendon and muscle injuries. Rat data shows faster return to baseline function following surgically induced muscle tears and tendon transections. No human performance studies exist. Most cited use case: recovery from overuse injuries and post-surgical healing acceleration.

citations.title: Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 enhances the growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts

safety.label: Preclinical — no human trial data
safety.adverse_events: No serious adverse events in animal studies across >100 publications
safety.regulatory: Not approved for human use. Research compound only.

citations.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 in trials for inflammatory bowel disease
Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 enhances the growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts
Modulatory effects of BPC 157 on vasomotor tone and Src-Caveolin-1-eNOS pathway
Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing

energy_generation.name: VEGF/HIF-1α Pathway
energy_generation.detail: Upregulates VEGF and HIF-1α, promoting angiogenesis and improved tissue oxygenation at repair sites.

growth.name: ILK/Akt Signaling
growth.detail: Integrin-linked kinase activation promotes cell survival and migration through Akt (PKB) pathway, critical for cardiac and musculoskeletal repair.

proliferation.name: Keratinocyte Migration
proliferation.detail: +42% increase in keratinocyte migration speed in scratch assays, directly accelerating wound closure.

proliferation.name: G-Actin Sequestration
proliferation.detail: Direct interaction with G-actin facilitates actin polymerization, enabling cell motility during tissue repair.

immune_function.name: Macrophage Polarization M1→M2
immune_function.detail: Shifts macrophage phenotype from pro-inflammatory M1 to regenerative M2, converting the tissue environment from damage to repair mode.

cytokines.name: IL-1β ↓
cytokines.detail: Interleukin-1 beta suppression reduces acute inflammatory tissue damage.

cytokines.name: TNF-α ↓
cytokines.detail: Tumor necrosis factor alpha reduction limits tissue destruction in inflammatory models.

human_use.summary: No peer-reviewed human clinical trials for TB-500 specifically have been published as of 2026. Not approved for human use by any regulatory agency. Naturally occurring Thymosin Beta-4 has been studied in a few human contexts (corneal healing), but the synthetic TB-500 fragment lacks clinical trial data.

animal_models.summary: Extensive preclinical data across multiple species and tissue types. Reduces cardiac infarct size and improves ejection fraction in mouse ischemia models. Accelerates wound closure and reduces scarring. Demonstrates macrophage polarization from M1 to M2. Hair follicle stimulation in animal models (density, regrowth). Long-distance tissue distribution from injection site due to low molecular weight.

citations.title: Thymosin β4 activates integrin-linked kinase and promotes cardiac cell migration, survival and cardiac repair
Multifunctional roles of Thymosin β4

performance.summary: Interest in athletic recovery primarily driven by cardiac and musculoskeletal repair data. Theoretical applications in reducing recovery time from muscle and tendon injuries. No human sports medicine studies published.

citations.title: Thymosin beta 4 accelerates wound healing

safety.label: Preclinical — no human trial data
safety.adverse_events: No significant adverse events in preclinical studies
safety.regulatory: Not approved for human use. Research compound only.

citations.title: Thymosin beta 4 accelerates wound healing
Thymosin β4 activates integrin-linked kinase and promotes cardiac cell migration
Multifunctional roles of Thymosin β4

metabolism.name: Lipolysis
metabolism.detail: GH-mediated increase in hormone-sensitive lipase activity promotes fat mobilization from adipose tissue.

energy_generation.name: Fat Oxidation
energy_generation.detail: Elevated GH drives preferential oxidation of fatty acids for energy, supporting body composition improvements.

growth.name: GH/IGF-1 Axis
growth.detail: Selective GHSR-1a agonist stimulates pulsatile GH release from anterior pituitary. GH triggers hepatic IGF-1 secretion, driving anabolic growth signals.

growth.name: Bone Formation
growth.detail: Increased longitudinal bone growth demonstrated in rat models via elevated GH/IGF-1 signaling. Enhanced bone mineral content in glucocorticoid-treated animals.

proliferation.name: Muscle Protein Synthesis
proliferation.detail: IGF-1 activation of PI3K/Akt/mTOR pathway increases skeletal muscle protein synthesis rate.

human_use.summary: Limited human pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic studies published. A Phase II clinical trial for postoperative ileus (NCT00272649) was initiated but discontinued. No published efficacy trials for GH deficiency or anti-aging indications. Two PK/PD studies documented GH response in healthy volunteers. Not FDA-approved.

citations.title: Pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling of ipamorelin, a growth hormone releasing peptide, in human volunteers

animal_models.summary: Robust data in rat models. Stimulates GH release with high selectivity — minimal effect on cortisol, prolactin, ACTH. Increases longitudinal bone growth. Reverses glucocorticoid-induced bone loss. Enhanced body composition in metabolic studies.

citations.title: Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue
Ipamorelin, a new growth-hormone-releasing peptide, induces longitudinal bone growth in rats

performance.summary: Widely used in combination with CJC-1295 in longevity and performance protocols. Theoretical benefits include improved body composition, enhanced recovery, and sleep quality via pulsatile GH release. No controlled human performance studies.

safety.label: Limited human data — preclinical safety established
safety.adverse_events: Well-tolerated in limited human studies; no serious adverse events in animals
safety.regulatory: Not FDA-approved. Research use only. Phase II trial discontinued.

citations.title: Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue
Pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling of ipamorelin
Ipamorelin induces longitudinal bone growth in rats

names.scientific: Modified GRF 1-29 with Drug Affinity Complex

pharmacokinetics.half_life: With DAC: 6–8 days | Without DAC: ~30 min

metabolism.name: Lipolysis via GH
metabolism.detail: Sustained GH elevation drives hormone-sensitive lipase activation, promoting visceral and subcutaneous fat mobilization.

energy_generation.name: Fat Oxidation
energy_generation.detail: GH-mediated metabolic shift toward fatty acid oxidation for fuel, sparing glucose and protein.

growth.name: GH/IGF-1 Axis (Sustained)
growth.detail: 2–10 fold mean GH elevation persisting throughout the week. IGF-1 increases 50–200% above baseline for up to 2 weeks. Drives anabolic signaling in muscle, bone, and connective tissue.

proliferation.name: Lean Mass Increase
proliferation.detail: Chronic GH/IGF-1 elevation activates mTOR pathway in skeletal muscle, increasing protein synthesis and lean body mass.

human_use.summary: Human PK/PD studies demonstrate dose-dependent sustained GH and IGF-1 elevation from single injection. Teichman et al. (2006) in JCEM established the 6–8 day half-life profile with DAC. Well-tolerated in healthy adults with injection site reactions and water retention as primary side effects. No long-term safety or efficacy trials published.

citations.title: Prolonged stimulation of growth hormone (GH) and insulin-like growth factor I secretion by CJC-1295

animal_models.summary: Synergistic GH release when combined with GHRP compounds (2–5× greater than either alone). Dual GHRH + ghrelin receptor pathway activation demonstrated. Dose-dependent IGF-1 increases in animal studies.

citations.title: CJC-1295 combined with GHRP-6: Synergistic effects on growth hormone secretion

performance.summary: Commonly combined with Ipamorelin in longevity and performance protocols. Extended GH/IGF-1 elevation from weekly dosing makes it convenient for sustained anabolic support. Theoretical improvements in body composition, recovery, and sleep. No controlled performance trials.

safety.label: Human PK data — limited safety profile
safety.adverse_events: Injection site reactions, transient water retention, headache
safety.regulatory: Not FDA-approved. Human PK studies only.

citations.title: Prolonged stimulation of GH and IGF-1 secretion by CJC-1295
CJC-1295 combined with GHRP-6 synergistic effects

metabolism.name: cAMP/PKA Cascade
metabolism.detail: GLP-1 receptor → Gs protein → adenylyl cyclase → cAMP elevation → PKA activation → glucose-dependent insulin secretion from beta cells.

metabolism.name: Insulin Secretion (Glucose-Dependent)
metabolism.detail: Stimulates insulin only in the presence of elevated glucose, minimizing hypoglycemia risk.

metabolism.name: Glucagon Suppression
metabolism.detail: Reduces glucagon secretion from alpha cells, lowering hepatic glucose output.

energy_generation.name: Gastric Emptying Delay
energy_generation.detail: Slowed gastric emptying reduces postprandial glucose spikes and promotes satiety via gut-brain axis.

growth.name: Beta-Cell Protection
growth.detail: Reduces glucotoxicity and lipotoxicity in pancreatic beta cells, potentially preserving insulin-secreting capacity.

cancer.name: Medullary Thyroid Carcinoma Risk (Rodent)
cancer.detail: C-cell hyperplasia and MTC observed in rodent studies at high doses. Human risk unclear; contraindicated in personal/family history of MTC or MEN2.

central_dogma.name: NASH Resolution
central_dogma.detail: Gene expression changes in hepatocytes reduce fat accumulation and inflammation. 59% NASH resolution at highest dose vs 17% placebo (Newsome et al. 2021).

immune_function.name: Anti-inflammatory (Systemic)
immune_function.detail: Reduces circulating inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6) independent of weight loss effects.

cytokines.name: CRP ↓
cytokines.detail: C-reactive protein reduction reflects reduced systemic inflammation.

cytokines.name: IL-6 ↓
cytokines.detail: Interleukin-6 reduction independent of weight loss.

human_use.summary: FDA-approved for type 2 diabetes (Ozempic, 2017) and obesity (Wegovy, 2021). Average weight loss of 14.9% at 2.4 mg weekly in STEP 1 trial (N=1,961, 68 weeks) — approaching bariatric surgery outcomes. SUSTAIN-6 showed 26% MACE reduction in T2D patients. SELECT trial (N=17,604) showed 20% MACE reduction in non-diabetic obesity. NASH resolution in 59% of patients at highest dose. Oral formulation (Rybelsus) FDA-approved 2019.

citations.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity
Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes
A Placebo-Controlled Trial of Subcutaneous Semaglutide in Nonalcoholic Steatohepatitis

animal_models.summary: Rodent studies show improved cognitive function, reduced brain inflammation, and decreased amyloid-β accumulation. C-cell hyperplasia at high doses in rodents — basis for MTC contraindication. Dose-dependent weight loss and metabolic improvements across species.

performance.summary: Not primarily studied for performance enhancement. Weight loss (14.9% average) improves functional capacity and reduces obesity-related comorbidities. SELECT trial showed MACE reduction in people with obesity but without diabetes — relevant for longevity. Tirzepatide achieves ~47% greater weight loss in head-to-head comparisons.

safety.label: FDA-approved — extensive safety database
safety.adverse_events: Nausea (most common, usually resolves), vomiting, gallbladder disease, rare pancreatitis, rare gastroparesis
safety.regulatory: FDA-approved: Ozempic (2017, T2D), Rybelsus (2019, T2D oral), Wegovy (2021, obesity). EMA approved.

citations.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity (STEP 1)
Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes (SUSTAIN-6)
Semaglutide in Nonalcoholic Steatohepatitis (NASH trial)

pharmacokinetics.half_life: Minutes in serum; CNS effects persist longer

dosing.notes: Primary clinical route; direct BBB access

energy_generation.name: Neuronal Metabolism
energy_generation.detail: Enhances cerebral metabolic rate and neuronal energy efficiency through BDNF-mediated mitochondrial optimization.

growth.name: BDNF Upregulation
growth.detail: Elevates brain-derived neurotrophic factor expression in hippocampus, cortex, and basal forebrain. Supports neurogenesis, synaptic plasticity, and long-term potentiation.

growth.name: NGF Upregulation
growth.detail: Nerve growth factor elevation supports cholinergic neuron maintenance and peripheral nerve health.

proliferation.name: Neurogenesis Support
proliferation.detail: BDNF-driven neurogenesis in hippocampal dentate gyrus supports new neuron formation and memory consolidation.

central_dogma.name: Gene Expression Modification
central_dogma.detail: Modulates hundreds of genes related to neuronal plasticity, neuroprotection, and synaptic function. Stavchansky et al. (2011) identified immune response gene regulation via microarray.

immune_function.name: Immunomodulatory
immune_function.detail: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects in CNS tissue. Reduces oxidative stress markers in brain. Regulates immune response genes.

effects.safety_label: Approved in Russia/Ukraine — no FDA/EMA approval

human_use.summary: Approved in Russia since 1994 and Ukraine for ischemic stroke treatment, cognitive impairment, optic nerve atrophy, and attention deficit disorders. Gusev et al. (2006) demonstrated improved neurological outcomes in acute ischemic stroke patients. Used clinically in Eastern Europe; not available in Western markets except as research compound.

citations.title: Semax in the treatment of patients with acute ischemic stroke

animal_models.summary: Extensive rodent data. Memory and learning improvements in multiple models. Stroke damage reduction and motor recovery. BDNF and NGF upregulation confirmed in hippocampus, cortex, and basal forebrain. Dopaminergic and serotonergic pathway activation via microdialysis analysis. Anxiolytic effects without sedation or dependence potential.

citations.title: Semax, an ACTH(4-10) analogue with nootropic properties, activates dopaminergic and serotoninergic brain systems

performance.summary: Used as a nootropic for cognitive performance, memory formation, attention, and executive function. Biohacking community reports improved focus and stress resilience. No controlled human performance studies. Russian clinical approval provides indirect evidence for cognitive efficacy in pathological states.

safety.label: Clinically approved in Russia/Ukraine; no FDA/EMA data
safety.adverse_events: Generally well-tolerated; nasal irritation from intranasal route
safety.regulatory: Approved Russia (1994), Ukraine. Not FDA/EMA approved.

citations.title: Semax activates dopaminergic and serotoninergic brain systems
Semax regulates expression of immune response genes
Semax in treatment of acute ischemic stroke

pharmacokinetics.half_life: SC ~105 min; topical — sustained tissue retention

dosing.notes: Primary studied route for skin/wound healing

metabolism.name: Antioxidant Enzyme Activation
metabolism.detail: Activates superoxide dismutase (SOD), catalase, and glutathione peroxidase — 2–4 fold increases. Reduces lipid peroxidation and DNA oxidation.

energy_generation.name: Cytochrome C Oxidase
energy_generation.detail: Copper ion essential for Complex IV (cytochrome c oxidase) activity — the final step of mitochondrial electron transport chain, directly powering ATP synthesis.

growth.name: Collagen Synthesis
growth.detail: Lysyl oxidase activation (copper-dependent) crosslinks collagen and elastin. 70–200% increase in collagen types I, III, and elastin in human dermal fibroblasts.

growth.name: Hair Follicle Enlargement
growth.detail: Dermal papilla cell proliferation, extended anagen phase, reduced DHT-mediated follicle miniaturization.

proliferation.name: Angiogenesis
proliferation.detail: Promotes blood vessel formation in wound beds — increased capillary density enhances nutrient delivery to healing tissue.

cancer.name: Metastatic Gene Suppression
cancer.detail: Connectivity Map analysis (Pickart 2012): suppresses 54 genes associated with the metastatic phenotype. 32% of human genome affected by GHK-Cu gene modulation.

central_dogma.name: Broad Gene Expression Modulation
central_dogma.detail: Affects 4,128 genes (~32% of human genome). Suppresses genes associated with inflammation, oxidative stress, and metastasis. Activates tissue repair, collagen synthesis, and cell survival genes.

immune_function.name: NF-κB Suppression
immune_function.detail: Inhibits NF-κB pathway activation, reducing transcription of pro-inflammatory genes.

cytokines.name: IL-6 ↓
cytokines.detail: Interleukin-6 reduction in dermal and systemic contexts.

cytokines.name: TNF-α ↓
cytokines.detail: Tumor necrosis factor alpha suppression via NF-κB inhibition.

cytokines.name: IL-1β ↓
cytokines.detail: Interleukin-1 beta reduction in inflammatory skin models.

human_use.summary: Double-blind, placebo-controlled 12-week trial (Abdulghani et al. 1998, n=71 women aged 50–70): 55% reduction in fine line depth, measurable increases in skin thickness and density via ultrasound imaging, enhanced procollagen I and III expression. Multiple smaller human studies confirm topical safety and tolerability. No systemic human trials for subcutaneous route.

citations.title: GHK-Cu peptide: double-blind clinical trial for skin aging

animal_models.summary: Wound contraction accelerated ~30% vs controls (Maquart et al. 1988). Increased capillary density in wound beds. Improved collagen organization. Hair follicle stimulation with density and regrowth improvements. Anti-fibrotic effects via TGF-beta modulation.

citations.title: Stimulation of collagen synthesis in fibroblast cultures by the tripeptide-copper complex glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine-Cu2+

performance.summary: Widely used in topical skincare for anti-aging. Systemic use in longevity protocols for collagen support, antioxidant defense, and hair health. The broad gene expression modulation (~32% of genome) is notable but requires further mechanistic characterization.

citations.title: GHK-Cu and gene expression modulation

safety.label: Clinical topical data — systemic use less studied
safety.adverse_events: Mild skin irritation at high topical concentrations; generally well-tolerated
safety.regulatory: Topical ingredient approved in cosmetics (EU, US). No systemic regulatory approval.

citations.title: Regenerative and Protective Actions of the GHK-Cu Peptide in the Light of the New Gene Data
GHK-Cu affects 4,128 genes including metastatic suppression
GHK-Cu skin aging clinical trial
Wound contraction acceleration by GHK-Cu

metabolism.name: Antioxidant Enhancement
metabolism.detail: Upregulates SOD, catalase, and glutathione peroxidase. 25–40% reduction in lipid peroxidation and protein carbonyls.

energy_generation.name: Mitochondrial Function
energy_generation.detail: Improved mitochondrial efficiency and reduced ROS production. Enhanced cellular energy metabolism in aging tissues.

proliferation.name: Telomere Lengthening
proliferation.detail: Telomerase activation leads to ~33% longer telomeres in treated fibroblasts. Overcomes Hayflick limit in cultured human cells.

cancer.name: Tumor Suppression (Animal)
cancer.detail: Delayed tumor onset in rodent longevity models. Reduced all-cause mortality in elderly observational studies.

central_dogma.name: Telomerase/TERT Epigenetic Activation
central_dogma.detail: 2–3 fold increases in telomerase expression via histone acetylation at TERT promoter. First reported mechanism for peptide-induced telomerase activation in human somatic cells (Khavinson 2003).

immune_function.name: Thymic Function Restoration
immune_function.detail: Restores CD4+/CD8+ T-cell populations in aging models. Improves adaptive immune response capacity.

effects.safety_label: Animal/in vitro — no independent human trials

human_use.summary: No independent Western peer-reviewed human clinical trials published. Khavinson's group (St. Petersburg) has published observational data suggesting reduced all-cause mortality in elderly subjects receiving periodic courses, but these studies have not been independently replicated. The majority of published research comes from a single laboratory, limiting evidence quality.

citations.title: Peptide bioregulation of aging: results and prospects
citations.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine

animal_models.summary: Mouse: 10–25% lifespan extension. Drosophila: 11–16% median survival increase. Delayed tumor onset and immune decline. 2.4-fold increase in telomerase activity in human fibroblasts (in vitro). Restores nocturnal melatonin to youthful levels (+50–100%). Reduces pineal gland lipofuscin accumulation.

citations.title: Epithalon peptide induces telomerase activity and telomere elongation in human somatic cells
citations.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine

citations.title: Effect of Epithalon on aging and lifespan in Drosophila

performance.summary: Used in longevity protocols for its theoretical anti-aging properties: telomere preservation, melatonin restoration, antioxidant enhancement. Popular in biohacking communities. Evidence quality is limited by single-lab origin and lack of independent replication.

safety.label: Preclinical — limited safety profile
safety.adverse_events: No serious adverse events reported in published studies
safety.regulatory: Not approved for human use. Research compound.

citations.title: Peptide bioregulation of aging
Epithalon induces telomerase activity in human somatic cells
Effect of Epithalon on aging in Drosophila

central_dogma.name: MC3R/MC4R Hypothalamic Activation
central_dogma.detail: Agonist at melanocortin MC3R and MC4R receptors in hypothalamus and limbic system. cAMP → PKA cascade activates neuronal circuits governing sexual desire — mechanism independent of vascular effects.

cytokines.name: Oxytocin Release
cytokines.detail: MC4R activation in paraventricular nucleus (PVN) of hypothalamus triggers oxytocin release — the 'bonding' neuropeptide — contributing to arousal and desire.

human_use.summary: FDA-approved June 2019 for hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD) in premenopausal women — the first subcutaneous HSDD treatment. RECONNECT Phase 3 trials (N>1,200) showed statistically significant increases in satisfying sexual events (SSEs) and reductions in distress related to low desire. Primary adverse effects: nausea (40%), flushing (20%), transient +6 mmHg systolic BP.

citations.title: Bremelanotide for Hypoactive Sexual Desire Disorder in Premenopausal Women: A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Dose-Finding Trial

animal_models.summary: Early animal studies identified the central (non-vascular) mechanism of action — distinct from PDE5 inhibitors. MC3R/MC4R activation in hypothalamus and limbic system mediates sexual behavior in rodent and primate models. Oxytocin release from PVN confirmed in animal studies.

citations.authors: Van der Ploeg LH, et al.
citations.title: A role for the melanocortin 4 receptor in sexual function

performance.summary: Primarily indicated for sexual function. Early Phase 1/2 male studies showed significant erectile response at 4–6 mg through CNS pathways (Wessells et al. 2005). Neuroimaging shows increased activity in anterior cingulate cortex and insula — brain regions associated with desire and motivation.

citations.title: PT-141: a melanocortin agonist for the treatment of sexual dysfunction

safety.label: FDA-approved — Phase 3 safety database
safety.adverse_events: Nausea 40%, flushing 20%, transient hypertension +6 mmHg systolic, focal hyperpigmentation with prolonged use
safety.regulatory: FDA-approved June 2019 (HSDD in premenopausal women). Brand: Vyleesi.

citations.title: Bremelanotide for HSDD — RECONNECT trials
PT-141 melanocortin agonist for sexual dysfunction

citations.authors: Van der Ploeg LH, et al.
citations.title: Melanocortin 4 receptor role in sexual function
